channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Azure Virtual Desktop in the Perth Azure Extended Zone: A Real‑World Production Deployment
item.description: Introduction Perth is one of the most geographically isolated major cities globally. For performance‑sensitive, graphics‑heavy engineering workloads, user experience can degrade significantly when desktops are hosted far from the user. For workloads such as subsurface modelling and GPU‑intensive...
item.dc:creator: akashmishra

item.title: How do I fix a malfunction in the Windows 11 shutdown update feature?
item.description: The shutdown option in the Start Menu is unresponsive, the update page cannot be opened, and both third-party repair attempts and manual patch installations have failed. How can this be resolved?
item.dc:creator: JacobBrown

item.title: How do I fix an abnormal screen and system freeze during Windows 11 startup?
item.description: After starting up, Win11 always enters an abnormal interface (e.g., blue screen/BIOS) and sometimes freezes or restarts. How can this be investigated?
item.dc:creator: CedricRichmond

item.title: Custom Text-to-Speech Voices on Windows 11
item.description: Can I replace Windows 11's built-in TTS voices with custom ones? Where are the voice files located?
item.dc:creator: GageWilder

item.title: How to get Windows 10 light blue color on Windows 11?
item.description: I want the light blue accent color from Windows 10. How to set it in Windows 11?
item.dc:creator: DaxxonFoster

item.title: Windows11 Prompts "Secure Boot KEK Update" Should I Install It?
item.description: The system showed a "Secure Boot KEK update" notification. What is this, and is it necessary to install?
item.dc:creator: Stonehu

item.title: How to restore the old Start Menu on Windows 11
item.description: The new Windows 11 Start Menu is awful. How can I revert it? I also want to remove the automatically added folders.
item.dc:creator: AnselRhodes

item.title: Cross‑tenant helpdesk impersonation to data exfiltration: A human-operated intrusion playbook
item.description: Threat actors are abusing external Microsoft Teams collaboration to impersonate IT helpdesk staff and convince users to grant remote access. Once inside, attackers can abuse legitimate tools and standard admin protocols to move laterally and exfiltrate data while appearing as routine IT...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Desktop icons slow to display (sometimes) on power on boot
item.description: Running Win11 Home, 24H2.Normally on a power on boot, I enter my Win11 password and press the ENTER key. The desktop icons are displayed within about a second.But in about once in 8 power on boots, I enter my Win11 password, press the ENTER key and it takes about 20 to 25 seconds for the desktop...
item.dc:creator: Holaway

item.title: Transfer MS Office 365 Business ownership from MSP to own MS Business account
item.description: Hello EverybodyI need to transfer my clients MS Office 365 Business from current MSP to their own MS Business account. Current MSP very bad service. So, at the end they want to manage their Emails\Licenses on MS Admin Center. What are the steps on doing this?
item.dc:creator: JacquesRossouw-ZA

item.title: Microsoft Fabric Operations Agent Step by Step Walkthrough
item.description: Fabric Capacity and Workspace You need a Microsoft Fabric workspace backed by a paid capacity. Trial capacities are not supported for Operations Agent. Your capacity must be provisioned in a supported region. As of April 2026, Operations Agent is available in all Microsoft Fabric regions except...
item.dc:creator: NaufalPrawironegoro

item.title: MCP Demystified: Tools vs Resources vs Prompts Explained Simply
item.description: Introduction When developers start working with Model Context Protocol (MCP), one of the most confusing parts is understanding the difference between MCP Tools, Resources, and Prompts. All three are important components in modern AI application development, but they serve completely different...
item.dc:creator: Ravindra_Kumar_Vishwakarma

item.title: Deploy scalable ring‑fenced Purview operations with Administrative Units
item.description: As Microsoft Purview deployments mature, many organisations encounter the same scaling challenge: how do you decentralize operations without fragmenting governance or losing visibility? Administrative Units (AUs) provide a native way to solve this by enabling ring‑fenced operations—allowing teams...
item.dc:creator: MaximeBombardier

item.title: GitHub Copilot CLI now supports Copilot auto model selection
item.description: Copilot auto model selection is now generally available in GitHub Copilot CLI for all Copilot plans. With auto, Copilot chooses the most efficient model on your behalf. How it works… The post GitHub Copilot CLI now supports Copilot auto model selection appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Which Entra account are you supposed to use to connect to a managed Google Play account?
item.description: At Connect Intune account to managed Google Play account - Microsoft Intune | Microsoft Learn, it says:We recommend using the Microsoft Entra account you're signed into to create the Google Admin account.So I used my Entra account to set it up. Now, though, when I look at the Managed Google Play...
item.dc:creator: RyanSteele-CoV

item.title: Autonomous AKS Incident Response with Azure SRE Agent: From Alert to Verified Recovery in Minutes
item.description: When a Sev1 alert fires on an AKS cluster, detection is rarely the hard part. The hard part is what comes next: proving what broke, why it broke, and fixing it without widening the blast radius, all under time pressure, often at 2 a.m. Azure SRE Agent is designed to close that gap. It connects...
item.dc:creator: hailukassa

item.title: Building an emoji list generator with the GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description: See how we created an emoji list generator during the Rubber Duck Thursday stream. The post Building an emoji list generator with the GitHub Copilot CLI appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Cassidy Williams

item.title: Bringing more transparency to GitHub’s status page
item.description: Changes to the status page will provide more specific data, so you'll have better insight into the overall health of the platform. The post Bringing more transparency to GitHub’s status page appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Jakub Oleksy

item.title: Bringing AI to the Factory Floor with Foundry Local - Now in Public Preview on Azure Local
item.description: Key capabilities in this preview Foundry Local exposes standard REST and OpenAI‑compatible APIs, enabling IT and AI teams to deploy and operate local AI workloads using familiar, cloud‑aligned patterns across edge and on‑prem environments. In this public preview, we deliver the following...
item.dc:creator: liranlyabock_microsoft

item.title: Containing a domain compromise: How predictive shielding shut down lateral movement
item.description: Domain compromise accelerates fast. Predictive shielding slowed it down. This real-world attack shows how exposure-based containment stopped credential abuse and broke the threat actor's momentum. The post Containing a domain compromise: How predictive shielding shut down lateral movement appeared...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Windows 11 Media Player Legacy still looks like it’s from the 2000s
item.description: I noticed the old Media Player Legacy on Windows 11 still has a 2000s-style interface. Is this kept on purpose by Microsoft?
item.dc:creator: FinneasHayes

item.title: Windows 11 Microsoft Edge opened automatically after system update
item.description: After my Windows 11 updated, Edge launched by itself, which never happened before. Startup launch is already disabled. Is this normal? How can I stop it?
item.dc:creator: KairosRivers

item.title: Windows 11 disk usage 100% even with no apps open, very laggy
item.description: My disk usage hits 100% right after boot with no apps running. The system is extremely slow, even opening a folder takes a long time. Checked for bad sectors and disabled Superfetch, still the same.
item.dc:creator: GageWilder

item.title: Windows 11 Start Menu won’t open, no response at all
item.description: Clicking the Start Menu does nothing, the Win key also doesn’t work. I’ve restarted File Explorer and rebuilt Start Menu cache, but it still won’t open.
item.dc:creator: EmmettBrooks

item.title: Comment supprimer definitivement des fichiers sur pc ? Besoin d’aide
item.description: Je cherche une façon sûre de supprimer des fichiers sensibles de mon PC pour qu’ils ne puissent pas être récupérés plus tard. J’ai déjà mis certains fichiers dans la Corbeille et je les ai supprimés, mais j’ai lu que cela ne les efface pas vraiment du disque. Comme je ne suis pas très à l’aise avec...
item.dc:creator: Zillmann

item.title: How to fix iphone black screen but it is still on and vibrates?
item.description: I'm a bit of a newbie with iPhones and I've run into a problem I can't figure out. My iPhone screen is completely black and won't wake up, but I know the phone is still on because it vibrates when I get a notification and I can hear the charging sound when I plug it in.I haven't dropped it or...
item.dc:creator: Obaileyy

item.title: Improving How Outlook Classic Handles Reactions
item.description: A fix due in May 2026 will make sure that the Outlook classic client will handle Outlook reactions properly if the reactions are blocked. Of course, if tenants don’t like responding to email with an emoji, they can create a transport rule to insert the x-ms-reactions header. Detecting the header is...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: How to speed up macbook air as it is very slow
item.description: My MacBook has become very slow lately, and I am not sure what to do to make it run faster. It takes a long time to start up, apps open slowly, and sometimes the whole system feels laggy even when I am only doing simple tasks like browsing the web or checking files.I am not very tech-savvy, so I...
item.dc:creator: Natrauss

item.title: ⚡Foundry Toolkit for VS Code: A Deep Dive on GA
item.description: As we shared in the announcement, Microsoft Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code is now generally available. In this deep dive, we walk through everything that’s in the GA release — from the rebrand and extension consolidation, to model experimentation, agent development, evaluations, and...
item.dc:creator: junjieli

item.title: 🏆 Agents League Winner Spotlight – Reasoning Agents Track
item.description: Agents League was designed to showcase what agentic AI can look like when developers move beyond single‑prompt interactions and start building systems that plan, reason, verify, and collaborate. Across three competitive tracks—Creative Apps, Reasoning Agents, and Enterprise Agents—participants had...
item.dc:creator: carlottacaste

item.title: I showed my son the GPT Image 2 leaks: Is the yellow filter finally gone?
item.description: My teenager can usually spot an AI image from across the room. He calls it the "OpenAI glow." You know the exact aesthetic I'm talking about. That weird, overly lit, slightly plastic yellow-orange filter that makes every single DALL-E 3 output look like a high-budget mobile game ad.So when the...
item.dc:creator: Azael123

item.title: Is it okay if I use Windows PowerShell for changing/adding recipes?
item.description: I'm currently dipping my toes into minecraft modding with basically no experience with coding or programming, I wanna know how important those numbers on the side of his stuff is or if I have to do anything with the text currently on my console (I think it's called that, correct me if I'm wrong).I...
item.dc:creator: Truetto

item.title: New Player, Having some trouble just getting started
item.description: I am just starting the game and as I was going through the beginning of the tutorial, I hadn't even gotten to the space port yet, and I ended up having to step away from my computer so I quit the game. Now when I try to continue the tutorial through the tutorial prompt it gives me I can't even load...
item.dc:creator: Colbybt

item.title: New in Azure SRE Agent: Log Analytics and Application Insights Connectors
item.description: Azure SRE Agent now supports Log Analytics and Application Insights as log providers, backed by the Azure MCP Server. Connect your workspaces and App Insights resources, and the agent can query them directly during investigations. Why This Matters Log Analytics and Application Insights are common...
item.dc:creator: Dalibor_Kovacevic

item.title: Event-Driven IaC Operations with Azure SRE Agent: Terraform Drift Detection via HTTP Triggers
item.description: What Happens After terraform plan Finds Drift? If your team is like most, the answer looks something like this: A nightly terraform plan runs and finds 3 drifted resources A notification lands in Slack or Teams Someone files a ticket During the next sprint, an engineer opens 4 browser tabs —...
item.dc:creator: Vineela-Suri

item.title: Introducing the plugin marketplace for Azure API Center
item.description: Today, we're excited to announce the public preview of the plugin marketplace endpoint for Azure API Center. This new capability makes it easier than ever for developers to discover and install AI plugins — including MCP servers and skills — directly from the tools they already use, like Claude...
item.dc:creator: Sreekanth_Thirthala

item.title: Best of Both Worlds for Agentic Refactoring: GitHub Copilot + MicroVMs via Docker Sandbox
item.description: Legacy codebases frequently contain hardcoded logic and complex build scripts that depend on specific filesystem structures, making them notoriously difficult to modernize in isolated environments. Docker Sandbox addresses this challenge through a bidirectional workspace sync that preserves the...
item.dc:creator: cindywang

item.title: Explaining what GitHub Copilot Modernization can (and cannot do)
item.description: In the last post, we looked at the workflow: assess, plan, execute. You get reports you can review and the agent makes changes you can inspect. If you don’t know, GitHub Copilot Modernization is the new agentic tool that supports you to in modernizing older applications. Could it support you with...
item.dc:creator: PabloLopes

item.title: Introducing the Container Network Insights Agent for AKS: Now in Public Preview
item.description: We are thrilled to announce public preview of Container Network Insights Agent - Agentic AI network troubleshooting for your workloads running in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS). The Challenge AKS networking is layered by design. Azure CNI, eBPF, Cilium, CoreDNS, NetworkPolicy, CiliumNetworkPolicy,...
item.dc:creator: chandanAggarwal

item.title: Bad registrar on 365 side
item.description: Hello, We created a Microsoft 365 tenant several years ago but never used the online email services.We added and verifed our domain, which was registered with Orange at the time, and then created some users manually to use PowerBI.Today, we begin to migrate to Exchange Online. We have installed AD...
item.dc:creator: Olasneek

item.title: Microsoft Foundry Toolkit for VS Code is Now Generally Available
item.description: We are thrilled to announce that the Microsoft Foundry Toolkit for VS Code, formerly AI Toolkit, is now Generally Available (GA)! From first model prompt to production‑grade AI agents, Foundry Toolkit lets you build, debug, and ship AI end to end without ever leaving VS Code. Same Product. New...
item.dc:creator: junjieli

item.title: What’s New in Microsoft Foundry Fine-Tuning | April 2026
item.description: April 2026 brings three major Reinforcement Fine-Tuning updates: Global Training for o4-mini with lower per-token rates across 12+ regions, new GPT-4.1 model graders for richer reward signals, and a comprehensive RFT best practices guide to help you ship specialized models faster. The post What’s...
item.dc:creator: Blanca Li

item.title: Manage agent skills with GitHub CLI
item.description: Agent skills are reshaping how developers work with AI coding agents. Today we’re launching gh skill, a new command in the GitHub CLI that makes it easy to discover, install,… The post Manage agent skills with GitHub CLI appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Free Your Terminal with Detached Mode in Aspire 13.2
item.description: Aspire 13.2 brings detached mode to the CLI. Run your AppHost in the background, free your terminal, and let coding agents start, inspect, and manage your app without blocking. The post Free Your Terminal with Detached Mode in Aspire 13.2 appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: James Newton-King

item.title: My PC doesn't show up in Devices on my Microsoft account.
item.description: I asked for tech support yesterday and the last message was this:If the issue still does not appear after 24 hours, the case will need to be handled by our Engineering team. To proceed, I will provide you with the contact link for the team. If you experience any issues, you can raise a ticket using...
item.dc:creator: Aleksandar1

item.title: App attach in Azure Virtual Desktop now supports Windows Server 2025 and Windows Server 2022
item.description: Managing applications in virtualized desktop and server environments has traditionally required IT teams to bake apps directly into base images, driving image sprawl, slower updates, and higher operational overhead. App attach in Azure Virtual Desktop changes that model by enabling applications to...
item.dc:creator: Michelle_Moya

item.title: How GitHub uses eBPF to improve deployment safety
item.description: Learn how Github uses eBPF to detect and prevent circular dependencies in its deployment tooling. The post How GitHub uses eBPF to improve deployment safety appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Lawrence Gripper

item.title: Building your cryptographic inventory: A customer strategy for cryptographic posture management
item.description: Learn how to build a comprehensive cryptographic inventory and strengthen quantum‑safe readiness using Microsoft Security tools, best‑practice lifecycle models, and partner solutions. The post Building your cryptographic inventory: A customer strategy for cryptographic posture management appeared...
item.dc:creator: Aviram Shemesh and Jennifer Rutzer

item.title: Rule insights dashboard and unified filter bar
item.description: Rule insights dashboard GitHub repository rulesets are powerful, but it hasn’t been easy to spot trends like spikes in blocked pushes during an incident or patterns in bypass activity without… The post Rule insights dashboard and unified filter bar appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: From AI to .NET: 20 VS Live! Las Vegas Sessions You Can Watch Now
item.description: In March 2026, developers came together at VS Live! Las Vegas for a full week of technical learning, hands-on exploration, and a lot of great conversations about where software development is headed next. From AI-assisted development to modern .NET, cloud-native apps, and developer productivity,...
item.dc:creator: Jim Harrer
